PostingRules.keywords: gitlab posting, mermaid, vcs-reply, bot prefix, comment format, line comment, discussion reply
PostingRules.Mission: Rules for posting review results to GitLab MR. Loaded ONLY when posting ظ¤ never during analysis or chat output. The arch-interrogation directive produces a chat report (ASCII/widget); this directive governs how that report is converted for GitLab consumption and posted as comments. Cognitive role: Posting Formatter. Takes comment candidates from arch-interrogation, converts diagrams to Mermaid (GitLab-native format), applies ≡اجû prefix, and emits JSON for vcs-reply.

BeliefState.Axiom: ALL diagrams posted to GitLab MUST be Mermaid fenced blocks (```mermaid ظخ```). GitLab renders Mermaid natively. ASCII art, emoji-tree diagrams, widget SVG, and inline images do NOT render in GitLab comments ظ¤ they are FORBIDDEN.
Every posted message body MUST start with ≡اجû ظ¤ so readers immediately recognise it as an automated note.

Posting shape depends on the artifact type, per arch-interrogation OutputFormat ┬د9: - **Spec / design-doc** ظْ ONE consolidated comment attached to file line 1 (the whole report, with Mermaid diagrams). - **Code** ظْ per-line comments at the specific changed lines (diff_refs from vcs-worktree). - **Reply** ظْ into the existing discussion thread (discussionId), never a new comment.
Before posting, re-check arch-interrogation's prior discussion awareness (AX_PRIOR_DISCUSSION_AWARENESS): drop any candidate already covered by an existing thread. Only post what is genuinely new.

DiagramConversion.Goal: Convert chat-format diagrams (ASCII/widget) to GitLab-native Mermaid.
DiagramConversion.Rules: 1. ASCII tree/graph ظْ Mermaid flowchart LR or TD (whichever fits the relationship direction). 2. Widget visualization ظْ Mermaid equivalent (flowchart for entity maps, sequenceDiagram for flows). 3. Keep ظëج7 nodes per diagram (same discipline as arch-interrogation AX_VISUALIZE_WHEN_RELATIONAL). 4. Use fixed classDef for entity state ظ¤ COPY AS-IS from arch-interrogation entity-map convention: - new ظْ green fill (#dafbe1), green stroke (#2da44e) - modified ظْ yellow fill (#fff8c5), yellow stroke (#bf8700) - deleted ظْ red fill (#ffebe9), red stroke (#cf222e) - related/outside MR ظْ gray fill (#f6f8fa), gray dashed stroke (#999) 5. Solid edges for in-change connections, dashed edges for outside-MR connections. 6. Every diagram gets a one-line caption in Russian.

CommentFormat.Section:
A standalone discussion (new thread, not tied to a code line). JSON shape:
```json {"body": "≡اجû ظخcontent with Mermaid diagramsظخ"} ```

A reply inside an existing discussion thread. JSON shape:
```json {"discussionId": "<full GitLab discussion ID>", "body": "≡اجû ظخreply contentظخ"} ```

A comment attached to a specific code line in the diff. JSON shape:
```json { "body": "≡اجû ظخcommentظخ", "position": { "baseSha": "<base_sha>", "startSha": "<start_sha>", "headSha": "<head_sha>", "newPath": "<file_path>", "newLine": <line_number> } } ```
Line rules (per arch-interrogation diff_refs from vcs-worktree): - added line ظْ `newLine` - removed line ظْ `oldLine` - context line ظْ BOTH `oldLine` and `newLine` - `oldPath` = `newPath` unless file was renamed.

A code suggestion to replace one line with another (TYPO probe ظْ suggestion candidate). JSON shape is a line-comment with the body following the suggestion format:
```json { "body": "≡اجû suggestion:-A+B", "position": { "baseSha": "<base_sha>", "startSha": "<start_sha>", "headSha": "<head_sha>", "newPath": "<file_path>", "newLine": <line_number> } } ```
Where `-A` is the existing text to replace and `+B` is the suggested replacement. Posted as a line-comment on the relevant code line.

PostingRules.CandidateTagging: Each candidate from arch-interrogation OutputFormat ┬د9 carries a `kind`: | kind | Meaning | Posting action | |---|---|---| | `new-line-comment` | New finding, not raised before | Post as line-comment on the relevant code line | | `reply-to-thread` | Already raised by another reviewer; I add substance | Post as reply-to-thread with the given discussionId | | `correction-reply` | Another reviewer's claim is wrong per AX_GROUNDED_IN_REPO | Post as reply-to-thread with correction | | `awaiting-my-reply` | Thread where @my_login is mentioned or my last message got a reply | Post as reply-to-thread; operator sees this separately in the report | | `suggestion` | TYPO probe found a typo or style issue | Post as line-comment with ```suggestion:-A+B format (see ┬دsuggestion) |

VcsReplySyntax.Command: # Post live ظ¤ the operator's checkbox approval in the skill's action menu IS the # confirmation, so we post directly. No dry-run step in the flow (the `--dry-run` # flag still exists in the CLI for manual debugging, but the skill does not use it). gennady vcs-reply --project=<group/project> --iid=<iid> --vcs-source=<host> < comments.json
VcsReplySyntax.Requirement: host from --vcs-source; project and iid from the MR ref (group/project!iid).

PostingRules.HaltConditions: | ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_NO_TOKEN` | GITLAB_PERSONAL_TOKEN not set | | `H_NO_CANDIDATES` | No comment candidates to post (all dropped by dedup or operator skip) |
